steps.title: Write the shifted function
steps.content:
Since $s(t)=r(t)+1$, substitute the expression for $r(t)$.
$$
s(t)=63t-3t^2+1=-3t^2+63t+1
$$

steps.title: Find the vertex input
steps.content:
For a quadratic $at^2+bt+c$, the vertex occurs at $t=-\frac{b}{2a}$. Here $a=-3$ and $b=63$, so
$$
t=-\frac{63}{2(-3)}=\frac{63}{6}
$$

steps.title: Evaluate at the vertex
steps.content: Substitute $t=\frac{63}{6}$ into $s(t)$. Since $63=6\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)$, $$ s\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)=63\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)-3\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)^2+1 $$ $$ s\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)=6\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)^2-3\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)^2+1 $$ $$ s\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)=1+3\bigl(\frac{63}{6}\bigr)^2 $$ The answer is B.
